title: mespilus monogyna var. lagenaria
intro: Mespilus monogyna var. lagenaria, also known as the wild pear or flowering ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the family Rosaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits associated with Mespilus monogyna var. lagenaria include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
